REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, RESPONDENT RAY YEGANEH NOTICE OF SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PETITIONER'S AMENDED CIVIL PETITION PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16 IS DENIED. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE FIRST AMENDED PETITION IS LARGELY INCOMPREHENSIBLE. NOTWITHSTANDING THE UNCERTAINTY OF THE PETITION, DEFENDANT FILED THIS MOTION SEEKING TO STRIKE THE PETITION AND 4 CAUSES OF ACTION THEREIN. DEFENDANT DOES NOT IDENTIFY THE PURPORTED CAUSES OF ACTION. DEFENDANT FURTHER DOES NOT IDENTIFY ANY CLAIMS UPON WHICH RELIEF MAY BE GRANTED, THAT DEFENDANT BELIEVES ARE SUBJECT TO ANTI-SLAPP. FOR EXAMPLE, DEFENDANT DOES NOT ADDRESS HOW THE CLAIM FOR PREMISES LIABILITY OR PRODUCT LIABILITY ARISES OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND WHAT THAT PROTECTED ACTIVITY MAY BE. DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION POINTS TO ALLEGATIONS ON PAGE 5 AND 6 OF THE FIRST AMENDED PETITION. THOSE ALLEGATIONS APPEAR TO STATE THAT A LIEN FOR A CERTAIN AMOUNT WAS RECODED ON A DATE CERTAIN AND THAT AN ABSTRACT OF JUDGMENT FOR A CERTAIN AMOUNT WAS RECORDED ON A DATE CERTAIN. DEFENDANT CITES TO NO AUTHORITY AND PROVIDES NO ARGUMENT TO SUPPORT THEIR ASSERTION THAT THE RECORDATION OF THIS LIEN OR RECORDATION OF THIS ABSTRACT OF JUDGEMENT IS PROTECTED ACTIVITY WITHIN THE MEANING ON CCP 425.16(E)(1)-(4). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER
Off Calendar Jan-13-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Petitioner In Pro Per STEPHANIE CHU present in Court (tel: 630-207-5362), assisted by Certified Mandarin Interpreter Aidong Ni, certification number 301135.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PETITIONER STEPHANIE CHU's DEMURRER.

The Court explains the tentative ruling to Petitioner Stephanie Chu. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:

Off calendar for department 302. Because this case concerns real property, all motions other than those involving the trial date must be noticed to be heard in department 501. Moreover, while the motion is largely incomprehensible, it seems clear that it was not filed and served with the notice required by Code of Civil Procedure 1005.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/HEK)

Notice Of Special Motion To Strike Petitioner'S Amended Civil Petition Pursuant To Ccp 425.16
Off Calendar Sep-30-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: In-Person Appearance

Stephanie Chu, petitioner in pro per
Shannon Kwei, Mandarin interpreter

Ms Chu is not awared of the tentative ruling prior to the hearing and did not notice the opposing party.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT RAY YEGANEH NOTICE OF SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PETITIONER'S AMENDED CIVIL PETITION PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16 is DENIED. The Court notes that the First Amended Petition is largely incomprehensible. Notwithstanding the uncertainty of the Petition, Defendant filed this motion seeking to strike the petition and 4 causes of action therein. Defendant does not identify the purported causes of action. Defendant further does not identify any claims upon which relief may be granted, that defendant believes are subject to ANTI-SLAPP. For example, Defendant does not address how the claim for premises liability or product liability arises out of protected activity and what that protected activity may be. Defendant's notice of Motion points to allegations on page 5 and 6 of the First Amended Petition. Those allegations appear to state that a lien for a certain amount was recoded on a date certain and that an abstract of judgment for a certain amount was recorded on a date certain. Defendant cites to no authority and provides no argument to support their assertion that the recordation of this lien or recordation of this abstract of judgement is protected activity within the meaning on CCP 425.16(e)(1)-(4).

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Special Motion To Strike Petitioner'S Amended Civil Petition Pursuant To Ccp 425.16
Off Calendar Aug-30-2024 Continued To Sep-30-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Prior to Open Court, Plaintiff in Pro Per Stephanie Chu is present assisted by Court certified Mandarin interpreter Aidong Ni. Ms. Chu speaks on her behalf while the Mandarin interpreter is in Dept. 505. Ms. Chu informs the staff attorney she does not contest the continuance date.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT RAY YEGANEH's Special Motion To Strike Petitioner'S Amended Civil Petition Pursuant To Ccp 425.16.

Continued to September 30, 2024 pending the disposition of the challenge for cause under CCP 170.1. Peremptory challenge under CCP 170.6 is denied as untimely filed 4 days before the hearing.

Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (501)

MOTION To Grant And Order To Remove An Illegal Lien On 182 Dublin St. Sf, Ca 94112

Stephanie Chu, petitioner in pro per, in-person appearance
Amanda Peeter, Mandarin interpretor
No other appearances

Ms. Chu is not awared of the tentative and did not notice opposing counsel of her appearance for today. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER STEPHANIE CHU MOTION TO GRANT AND ORDER TO REMOVE AN ILLEGAL LIEN ON 182 DUBLIN ST. SF, CA 94112 is DENIED. Moving papers are devoid of any authority or facts supporting the requested relief.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
